question:
Study the information given below carefully

and answer the following question.

An uncertain number of people are seated in a

row facing north. S is seated fourth from the left

end. M is seated fourth to the right of S. Three

people are seated between M and D. L is

seated second to the left of G. P sits exactly

between S and M. O is seated fourth to the left

of L. A is seated at the one of the extreme ends

of the row. Three people are seated between D

and O. K is seated second to the right of M. E is

seated exactly between K and D. No one is

seated to the right of G.

Who is seated seventh to the right of A?

question:
Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

Nine people C, Z, H, W, M, K, S, Q, and F are

seated in a straight line facing North but not

necessarily in the same order. W and H are

sitting at the extreme ends. C is seated second

to the left of H. S is seated third to the right of

M. Neither of the two people seated between K

and H is M or S. H and F are not neighbors. Q is

seated third to the right of W.

How many persons are seated between M and

Z?

Nine people C, Z, H, W, M, K, S, Q, and F are

seated in a straight line facing North but not

necessarily in the same order. W and H are

sitting at the extreme ends. C is seated second

to the left of H. S is seated third to the right of

M. Neither of the two people seated between K

and H is M or S. H and F are not neighbors. Q is

seated third to the right of W.

Who among the following is seated between S

and C?
explanation:
W and H are sitting at the extreme ends

C is seated second to the left of H

Neither of the two people seated between K

and H is M or S

Q is seated third to the right of W

S is seated third to the right of M

Q is seated third to the right of W

H and F are not neighbors

This is the final arrangement.

question:
Direction : A certain number of people are

sitting in a row facing the north direction. Three

people are sitting between G and V. J sits fifth

to the right of O. M sits third to the left of K.

More than 10 people are sitting between M and

J. Six people are sitting between V and M. Eight

people are sitting between J and K. V sits fourth

from an end.

How many people are sitting between G and K?

questionHtml:
Direction : A certain number of people are

sitting in a row facing the north direction. Three

people are sitting between G and V. J sits fifth

to the right of O. M sits third to the left of K.

More than 10 people are sitting between M and

J. Six people are sitting between V and M. Eight

people are sitting between J and K. V sits fourth

from an end.

How many people are sitting between G and K?
explanation:
1. J sits fifth to the right of O.

2. Eight people are sitting between J and K.

3. M sits third to the left of K.

4. More than 10 people are sitting between M

and J.

(Here, case 1 will be eliminated)

5. Six people are sitting between V and M.

6. V sits fourth from an end.

7. Three people are sitting between G and V.

Hence, the required answer is 5.

question:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions accordingly.

Nine friends Tina, Parul, Anjali, Charu, Kritika,

Sanjana, Khushi, Vanika, and Tanya are sitting in

a straight line facing to the north but not

necessarily in the same order. Sanjana sits at

the extreme end of the row. Three persons sit

between Sanjana and Khushi. Parul is seated

exactly between Sanjana and Vanika. Vanika

sits left of Khushi. Parul does not sit to the right

of Khushi. Tanya is the only immediate neighbor

of Kritika. More than one person sits between

Tina and Charu who does not sit near Khushi.

Four of the following five are alike in a certain

way and hence form a group. Find out the one

that does not belong to that group.

two
adjacent persons face

the immediate left of both L and J. M and R

face the same direction.

Find the odd one.

Six persons U, V, W, X, Y and Z sit in a straight

row facing north. Each of them has different

number of rings (11, 12, 13, 17, 18 and 20) but not

necessarily in the same order.

Two persons sit to the left of the one, who has

12 rings. Z sits 2
 to the right of the person, who

has 20 rings. Person with 18 rings sits to the right

of Z. Y sits immediate left of the one, who has 18

rings but not adjacent to Z. Y has one ring more

than Z. U sits to the right of V, who sits to the

right of the person with 11 rings. X doesn’t have

11 rings.

How many persons sit to the right of W?

Case II: Three persons sit to the right of Z:

Inference:

From clue 6, U has 18 rings, V has 17 rings.

From clue 7, X has 20 rings, W has 11 rings.

The final seating arrangement is given below:

Four persons sit to the right of W.

question:
Study the following information carefully and

answer accordingly.

There are seven people named Suman, Rajat,

Parul, Vansh, Mahesh, Karan, and Lalit are born

in seven different years i.e. 1966, 1972, 1977, 1985,

1999, 2002, and 2010 but not necessarily in the

same order. The age is calculated according to

the year 2022. They are sitting in a row facing

the north direction. Suman and Karan are not

neighbors. The age difference of Vansh and

Mahesh is a number that is the reversal of the

last two digits of one of the given years. Three

people are sitting between the people who are

23 and 12 in age. More than one person is elder

to Vansh. The one, who has least value of the

addition of all the place values of the letters in

the name sits third from the left end. The

number of people sitting between Karan and

the one, who is 50 years old, is an even number.

Mahesh sits three places away from the one,

who is 20 years old. The one, who has the

maximum value of the addition of all the place

values of the letters in the name sits immediate

right of the one, who has a perfect cube of the

addition of all the place values of the letters in

the name. Vansh is not sitting to the right of the

one, who is 20 years old. One person is sitting

between Suman and Lalit, who is not the

youngest. The age difference of Suman and

Rajat is a perfect square. The one, who has

maximum number of consonants in the name

sits second from the right. The one, who has the

maximum value of the addition of all the place

values of the letters in the name is not the

eldest.

Find the odd one.

textPlain:
Study the following information carefully and

answer accordingly.

question:
Direction : A certain number of people are

sitting in a row facing the north direction. Three

people are sitting between G and V. J sits fifth

to the right of O. M sits third to the left of K.

More than 10 people are sitting between M and

J. Six people are sitting between V and M. Eight

people are sitting between J and K. V sits fourth

from an end.

If T sits exactly between K and O then how

many people are sitting between T and J?
question_html:
Direction : A certain number of people are

sitting in a row facing the north direction. Three

people are sitting between G and V. J sits fifth

to the right of O. M sits third to the left of K.

More than 10 people are sitting between M and

J. Six people are sitting between V and M. Eight

people are sitting between J and K. V sits fourth

from an end.

If T sits exactly between K and O then how

many people are sitting between T and J?
questionHtml:
Direction : A certain number of people are

sitting in a row facing the north direction. Three

people are sitting between G and V. J sits fifth

to the right of O. M sits third to the left of K.

More than 10 people are sitting between M and

J. Six people are sitting between V and M. Eight

people are sitting between J and K. V sits fourth

from an end.

If T sits exactly between K and O then how

many people are sitting between T and J?
explanation:
1. J sits fifth to the right of O.

2. Eight people are sitting between J and K.

3. M sits third to the left of K.

4. More than 10 people are sitting between M

and J.

(Here, case 1 will be eliminated)

5. Six people are sitting between V and M.

6. V sits fourth from an end.

information given below.

Certain numbers of persons were seated in a

straight row facing the north. The information

about only few of them is known.

F was seated 2
 to the left of A. Three persons

were seated between A and B. B was to the

right of F. E was 2
 to the left of B. D was to the

immediate right of B. C was at the extreme

right end. Only 1 person was seated between C

and D. Number of persons seated to the right of

B was equal to the number of persons seated

to the left of A.

How many persons were seated between C

and E?

question:
Study the following data carefully and answer

the questions accordingly.

Ten people sit in a two-parallel row. Five people

in each row. In row-1 A, B, C, D and E are seated

and all face towards south. In row-2 P, Q, R, S

and T are seated and all face towards the

north. People seated in row-1 face people

seated in row-2. S sits in the middle of the row.

The immediate neighbor of S faces E. E sits

three places away from B. T sits diagonally

opposite to B. A faces P. C and Q does not sit

on any of the extreme ends of the row. D sits

right of B.

Four of the following five are alike in a certain

way based on their positions in the above

arrangement and so form a group. Which of the

following does not belong to the group ?

question:
Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer accordingly.

In a society, there are ten houses from west to

east and numbered 1 to 10. The house in the

west end is numbered 1 and the house in the

east end is numbered 10. Eight persons with

different ages live in these houses (one person

in one house) and two houses are vacant.

There are 3 houses between S and K, who is 35

years old. Three people live between K and the

one, whose age is 49 years, who does not live in

house 10. Two people live between R and G,

whose age is not a perfect square. The age

difference of R and J is 39 years. The age

difference of L and J is 15 years. One of them is

Q and one of them is 51 years old. S lives in the

west of J but not immediately. There are 5

houses between the persons who are 49 years

old and 25 years old. The age difference of J

and K is more than 10. House 4 is not vacant.

There are 4 houses between the vacant houses.

J, whose age is a perfect square number, lives

in an odd-numbered house to the west of

house 7 (that is not occupied by K). Three

people live between the vacant house and the

one, who is 59 years old. L lives near P, who

does not live near the vacant house and is 6

years older than L.

Find the correct statement.

question:
Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

There are eight people A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H

sitting in a row. Some of them are facing north

and some of them are facing south but not

necessarily in the same order. D sits third to the

left of N, who sit at any of the extreme ends of

the row. There are two people sitting between

D and O. C sits second to the left of O and both

are facing opposite directions to each other. A

sits second to the right of C. M sits third to the

left of A, and both facing the same directions to

each other. M facing north. B sits left of L, who

does not sit at the end of the row. B and L

facing opposite directions to each other. D

facing south.

If O is related to C and A is related to N in a

certain way. Then, who among the following is

related to L?

Answer the questions based on the information

given below.

Nine cars (A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, and I) of different

costs (in lakhs) (7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 14, 15 and 17) are

parked in a parking lot, such that some cars are

facing north, and some cars are facing south

but not necessarily in the same order. Not more

than three cars face south direction.

Note: To distinguish between the left and right

ends of the row, take the ends as per north

direction but it doesn’t necessarily mean that

the cars at ends are facing north direction. The

distance between each adjacent car is same.

The 3 costliest car faces north and is parked

4 to the right of F. E is costlier than D but

cheaper than F. Four cars are parked between

the costliest car and D, which faces north. C,

which is the 3 cheapest car, is parked 3 to

the left of D. A, which is the 2
 costliest car, is

parked adjacent to C. The costliest car is

parked 3 to the left of the cheapest car. The

cheapest car is parked 4 from one of the

extreme ends. Two cars are parked between A

and E, which faces south. B, which faces south

is parked 4 to the left of E. H, which costs 11

lakhs, is parked adjacent to B. F, which faces

opposite direction as E, is not parked adjacent

to H or E. I is not the cheapest car.

question:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions accordingly.

Eight persons P, Q, R, S, T, U, V, and W are sitting

in the linear row such that all are facing the

north direction but not necessarily in the same

order. All persons have gift boxes of different

colours viz., Red, Green, Yellow, Blue, White,

Black, Pink, and Violet but not necessarily in the

same order.

The one who has a yellow colour gift box sits

two places away from the one who has a violet

colour box. W has a violet colour box and sits

on any one of the extreme ends of the row. V

sits immediate left of Q. Only one person sits

between T and S, who has a black colour box. R

sits at the end of the row and has a blue colour

box. U has the pink colour box and sits in the

middle of S and T. T has the red colour box and

sits on the right of U. The one who has a green

colour box sits adjacent to W. P is an

immediate neighbour of S. At least 3 persons sit

between the one who has a violet colour box

and the one who has a black colour box. Q

does not have a green colour box. P does not

have a yellow colour box and is not an

immediate neighbour of R. S is not an

immediate neighbour of R.

Who among the following person is an

immediate neighbour of W?

textPlain:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions accordingly.

question:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions accordingly.

Eight persons- A, B, C, D, E, F, G, and H are

sitting in the linear row. Four of them are facing

north and four of them are facing south but not

necessarily in the same order. Not more than

two persons facing the same direction sit

together and all of them have a different

number of novels from 1 to 10.

B, who has 2 novels sits third to the right of D,

and Vice versa. Only one person sits between D,

who has odd number of novels but less than 5

and C. B and C are not immediate neighbors.

The number of novels C has is square of the

number of novels that B has. A sits adjacent to

C and sits at end of the row. G, who has a prime

number of novels but not (2 and 7) sits second

to the right of A, and vice versa. A has three

novels less than H. The persons who are sitting

at the end are facing the opposite direction.

The number of persons sitting between E and F

is the same as between C and D. H has the

maximum number of
novels that can be

possible. F does not face the north direction. C

is facing the opposite direction of F. Immediate

neighbors of G facing the same direction. F and

E have 8 and 6 novels respectively. No one has

only 1 novel.

How many persons are seated to the left of D?

to the right of A, and vice versa. A has three

novels less than H. The persons who are sitting

at the end are facing the opposite direction.

The number of persons sitting between E and F

is the same as between C and D. H has the

maximum number of novels that can be

possible. F does not face the north direction. C

is facing the opposite direction of F. Immediate

neighbors of G facing the same direction. F and

E have 8 and 6 novels respectively. No one has

only 1 novel.

Who
among
the
following
person
sits

immediate left of the one who has 6 novels?
questionHtml:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions accordingly.

Eight persons- A, B, C, D, E, F, G, and H are

sitting in the linear row. Four of them are facing

north and four of them are facing south but not

necessarily in the same order. Not more than

two persons facing the same direction sit

together and all of them have a different

number of novels from 1 to 10.

B, who has 2 novels sits third to the right of D,

and Vice versa. Only one person sits between D,

who has odd number of novels but less than 5

and C. B and C are not immediate neighbors.

The number of novels C has is square of the

number of novels that B has. A sits adjacent to

C and sits at end of the row. G, who has a prime

number of novels but not (2 and 7) sits second

to the right of A, and vice versa. A has three

novels less than H. The persons who are sitting

at the end are facing the opposite direction.

The number of persons sitting between E and F

is the same as between C and D. H has the

maximum number of novels that can be

possible. F does not face the north direction. C

is facing the opposite direction of F. Immediate

neighbors of G facing the same direction. F and

E have 8 and 6 novels respectively. No one has

only 1 novel.

Who
among
the
following
person
sits

immediate left of the one who has 6 novels?
explanation:
H sits immediate left of the one who has 6

novels

Final Answer:

A sits adjacent to C and sits at end of the row.

Only one person sits between D, who has odd

number of novels but less than 5 and C.

B and C are not immediate neighbors.

No one has only 1 novel.

From the above conditions, we get,

B, who has 2 novels sits third to the right of D,

and Vice versa. 

The number of novels C has is the square of the

number of novels that B has.

G, who has a prime number of novels but not (2

and 7) sits second to the right of A, and vice

versa.

The persons who are sitting at the end are

facing the opposite direction.

The number of persons sitting between E and F

is the same as between C and D.

F does not face the north direction. C is facing

the opposite direction of F.

question:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions accordingly.

Nine women AM, BN, CO, DP, EQ, FR, GS, HT,

and IU, are sitting in a straight line with some

facing north and others facing south, but not

necessarily in the same sequence. There are no

more than two adjacent women facing the

same direction. The alphabetically named

women are not sitting next to each other.

Only five women are sitting between FR and CO

where both of them face the same direction but

do not sit on any of the ends. HT sits second to

the left of FR and faces the opposite direction

as FR. IU sits adjacent to both BN and GS where

both BN and GS face the same direction but

opposite direction as IU. DP faces towards the

north and does not sit on the ends of the row.

EQ sits to the right of DP and faces the same

direction as AM.

Four of the next five are similar in some way and

thus form a group. Find the one who does not

belong to that group.

question:
Directions:Read the information given below

carefully 
and 
answer 
the 
questions

accordingly.

There are six persons - Sharad, Paras, Rahul,

Himanshu, Abhishek, and Akhil seated in a row

facing north. They all are working professionals

viz. Clerk, Accountant, Teacher, Manager,

Branch manager, and CEO but not necessarily

in the same order.

The one who is working as CEO is seated at

one of the extreme ends of the row. Three

persons are seated between the one working

as CEO and Accountant. Rahul is seated third

from the left end of the row. Sharad is working

as a CEO. The one who working as Manager is

immediate neighbor of the one who is working

as CEO. Paras is seated at the extreme end of

the row. Abhishek is working as a Teacher. Akhil

is not working as an Accountant. Paras is not

working as Clerk. Rahul is seated to the right of

Sharad.

Who among the following is seated third to the

right of Akhil?

question:
The cheapest car is parked 4th from one of

the extreme ends.
question_html:
The cheapest car is parked 4th from one of

the extreme ends.
questionHtml:
The cheapest car is parked 4th from one of

the extreme ends.
explanation:
1. J, whose age is a perfect square number, lives

in an odd-numbered house to the west of

house 7 (that is not occupied by K).

2. S lives in the west of J but not immediately.

3. There are 3 houses between S and K, who is

35 years old.

4. Three people live between K and the one,

whose age is 49 years, who does not live in

house 10.

(That means:

· In case 1, one of the houses to the west of K is

vacant.

· In case 2, all the houses are occupied between

K and the house 9.)

5. There are 5 houses between the persons who

are 49 years old and 25 years old.

6. The age difference of J and K is more than 10.

(Here, case 2 will be eliminated)

7. House 4 is not vacant.

(That means house 3 must be vacant)

8. There are 4 houses between the vacant

houses.

9. Three people live between the vacant house

and the one, who is 59 years old.

10. L lives near P, who does not live near the

vacant house and is 6 years older than L.

11. Two people live between R and G, whose

age is not a perfect square.

12. The age difference of R and J is 39 years.

13. The age difference of L and J is 15 years.

(Refer point 10)

14. One of them is Q and one of them is 51 years

old.

Hence, five people live to the east of G.

A, which is the 2nd costliest car, is parked

adjacent to C.
question_html:
A, which is the 2nd costliest car, is parked

adjacent to C.
questionHtml:
A, which is the 2nd costliest car, is parked

adjacent to C.
explanation:
1. J, whose age is a perfect square number, lives

in an odd-numbered house to the west of

house 7 (that is not occupied by K).

2. S lives in the west of J but not immediately.

3. There are 3 houses between S and K, who is

35 years old.

4. Three people live between K and the one,

whose age is 49 years, who does not live in

house 10.

(That means:

· In case 1, one of the houses to the west of K is

vacant.

· In case 2, all the houses are occupied between

K and the house 9.)

5. There are 5 houses between the persons who

are 49 years old and 25 years old.

6. The age difference of J and K is more than 10.

(Here, case 2 will be eliminated)

7. House 4 is not vacant.

(That means house 3 must be vacant)

8. There are 4 houses between the vacant

houses.

9. Three people live between the vacant house

and the one, who is 59 years old.

10. L lives near P, who does not live near the

vacant house and is 6 years older than L.

11. Two people live between R and G, whose

age is not a perfect square.

12. The age difference of R and J is 39 years.

13. The age difference of L and J is 15 years.

(Refer point 10)

14. One of them is Q and one of them is 51 years

old.

Hence, five people live to the east of G.
